config.accelerator: cpu (AMD Radeon Pro VII present but no NVIDIA GPU; CUDA unavailable)
config.note: pool=10 used instead of 50 to keep CPU runtime under 3min per run. Both arms use identical pool.

diagnosis.root_cause: entity extraction treats stopwords (I, my, the) as entities; 250/250 memories contain 'i', 246/250 contain 'my'. This makes shared-entity edges near-complete graph, so activation spreading gives every node the same bonus.

conclusion.booster_mechanism_works: booster correctly reads edges, spreads activation, applies bonus to activation_bonus field
conclusion.performance_fix_applied: edge_weights_between rewritten from N*N per-row queries to batched IN-clause query (eliminated O(n^2) SQLite roundtrips)
conclusion.blocking_issue: entity extraction too coarse for dialog data - needs stopword filtering or named-entity-only extraction
conclusion.next_step: fix entity extraction quality, then rerun A/B; if edges become sparse enough to discriminate, activation should shift rankings
